observations.summary: The walking stick found by Dan, made of ash wood fire-hardened at the tip and smooth from years of use, parallels Aviel's past as a scholar who walked to houses of learning and sought counsel, symbolizing the legacy and burden passed from father to son.

source_evidence.quote: By the hearth, where the last coals still glowed a dull, sullen red, Dan found his father's old walking stick. Ash wood, fire-hardened at the tip, smooth from years of use. Aviel had carried it when he was still a scholar, back when he'd walk to the houses of learning, when men sought his counsel.

observations.target_hint: The walking stick symbolizes the weight of Aviel's scholarly past and the legacy Dan inherits, reflecting themes of burden, knowledge, and the passage of time.
